observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General
Services Office to prepare and submit the SSMI for Calendar Year
2022, pursuant to Section 121 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Management require concerned job
order workers to submit their individual accomplishment
reports duly verified and signed by their immediate supervisors,
in adherence to Section 4 (6) of Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445,
otherwise known as the “Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines” and Section 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001
dated 14 June 2012.. This is to ensure that the paid wages of the
job order workers were rendered in accordance with their
assigned tasks and their services were truly necessary.

We recommend that the Accounting Unit:
a.) Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry/s to remove from the
property, plant and equipment (PPE) account the semi-expendable
properties totaling ₱ 500,570.60;
b.) Report the same as part of its change in accounting policy; and
c.) Apply the new capitalization threshold of at least ₱ 50,000.00 as
part of its PPE on its current and future acquisitions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a.) The Municipal Assessor to prepare and submit to the Municipal
Treasurer on or before the 31st day of December each year, an
assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties
have been newly assessed or reassessed and the values of such
properties.
b.) The Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a
duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount
due and collectible for the current year. The real property tax
receivable and special education tax receivable should be
established not exceeding 1% of the assessed value of real property
appearing in the Real Property Tax Account Register or
Taxpayer’s index card.
c.) The Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to record
any prior years’ annual Real Property Tax receivable and Special
Education Tax receivable. It is further recommended that journal
entry voucher be prepared at the beginning of the year thereafter
to establish the current year’s receivable for Real Property Tax and
Special Education Tax.

We recommended that the BAC prepares the Procurement Monitoring
Report (PMR) with the approval by the Head of the Procuring Entity
and submit the same to the GPPB in printed and electronic format
within 14 calendar days after the end of each semester and the Annual
Procurement Plan to GPPB on or before end of January of the budget
year.

We recommend that the Management should follow the uniform property identification number system for Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) wherein a unique property number shall be assigned for each PPE item, in accordance with Paragraph 5.6 of COA Circular 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020.

We recommend that the agency should require the Supply/Property Officer to secure his/her fidelity bonding from the Bureau of Treasury, in compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated April 25, 2019.

We recommend that the Management:
a. Require all concerned agency officials/personnel to strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on the submission of financial reports/records including all supporting documents thereof within the prescribed deadlines to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the period when those transactions have transpired, in compliance with Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines; and
b. Enforce the penal provision on withholding of salaries of responsible officers in case of unjustified failure on their part to submit the reports on time.

We recommend that management officials shall comply with documentary requirements and strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit, pursuant to Section 9.1 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) as prescribed under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommend that the accountable officer prepare and submit the said Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms in the prescribed form provided in Section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated July 3, 1992 and Section 4(h), Volume I of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the audit team.

3.4 We recommend that the management should follow the uniform property
identification number system for Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)
wherein a unique property number shall be assigned for each PPE item, in
accordance with Paragraph 5.6 of COA Circular 2020-006, dated January
31, 2020.

4.5 We recommend that the agency should require the Supply/Property Officer
to secure his/her fidelity bonding from the Bureau of Treasury, in
compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated April 25, 2019.

5.6 We recommend that the Management:
a. Require all concerned agency officials/personnel to strictly comply with
the pertinent rules and regulations on the submission of financial
reports/records including all supporting documents thereof within the
prescribed deadlines to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the
period when those transactions have transpired, in compliance with
Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines; and
b. Enforce the penal provision on withholding of salaries of responsible
officers in case of unjustified failure on their part to submit the reports
on time.

6.6 We recommend that management officials shall comply with documentary
requirements and strictly follow the rules and regulations on the
disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension
in audit, pursuant to Section 9.1 of the Rules and Regulations on the
Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) as prescribed under Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

11.5 We recommend that the accountable officer prepare and submit the said
Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms in the
prescribed form provided in Section 37 of COA Circular No. 92-382 dated
July 3, 1992 and Section 4(h), Volume I of the Government Accounting
Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units, and submit the same not
later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the audit team..

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare, update the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting of the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account – 111. She should make a representation, with the approval of the Municipal Mayor, to the agency depository bank for the timely release of the bank statements in order that she can prepare, update and submit the BRS on time. The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, should see to it that this requirement should be complied by the Municipal Accountant being the responsible officer to prepare the BRS. Moreover, she should initiate efforts to address the problem to the latter by reducing her workloads and delegate them to other personnel capable of doing the job in the finance department in accordance with Section 74 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines, Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-125A dated March 4, 1992 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend that the municipality shall observe the provisions as prescribed under Sections 182, 187 and 189 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM), Volume I for Local Government Units (LGUs). The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer shall see to it that the SLC and SC are accurate 2. Physical inventory count must be regularly conducted and must be reconciled with the Supples Ledger Cards for accurate accounting and recording of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The LGU must conduct physical inventory count regularly and must be reconciled with Supplies Ledger Cards for accurate accounting and recording of inventories.
observations.reason: 1. The noted purchases were fully consumed / Physical count issued at the end of fiscal year; thus, reconciled with the account was reflected in General Ledger and the Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the accounts with a reported carrying value create Inventory Committee to conduct Physical Count of PPE and prepare journal entries to effect the changes, pursuant to Section 124, Volume I and Section 57, Volume II of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS), for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate ledgers for premiums and various types of loans for the purpose of monitoring the contributions and loan payments to PAG-IBIG to monitor the actual amounts due; (b) make sure that the amount withheld for a given month is remitted in full within the prescribed period so that members can enjoy optimum benefits due them, pursuant to Section 2, Rule VII of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9679 on the Home Development Mutual Law of 2009.

We recommend and management agreed to: a) Instruct the In Charge of Office (ICO) – Municipal Treasurer, together with other concerned offices to (i) intensify the collection of real property taxes, including other revenue sources to generate more income for the Municipality; (ii) post the notice of the delinquency in publicly accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the Municipality; (iii) publish the notice of delinquency once a week or for two consecutive weeks in a newspaper of general circulation of the Municipality. Proper dissemination would help to inform concerned taxpayers of their tax obligations and ultimately make them aware of their duty to pay in accordance with Section 57 of Presidential Decree (P.P) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines. b) Avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to enhance the collection of real property tax and enforce necessary administrative action thru levy on real property or judicial action as a last resort. c) Remind the In Charge of Office (ICO) – Municipal Treasurer his accountabilities in case of failure to collect, pursuant to Section 259 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Instruct the In Charge of Office (ICO) – Municipal Treasurer, together with other concerned offices to (i) intensify the collection of real property taxes, including other revenue sources to generate more income for the Municipality; (ii) post the notice of the delinquency in publicly accessible and conspicuous places in each barangay of the Municipality; (iii) publish the notice of delinquency once a week or for two consecutive weeks in a newspaper of general circulation of the Municipality. Avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to enhance the collection of real property tax and enforce necessary administrative action thru levy on real property or judicial action as a last resort. Remind the In Charge of Office (ICO) – Municipal Treasurer his accountabilities in case of failure to collect, pursuant to Section 259 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the LGU to adhere to paragraph 4.1-4.3 of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget Circular No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 and submit utilization reports to the Audit Team such as Disbursement Vouchers (DVs), Report of Check Issued (RCIs) and other related reports for audit purposes.
observations.reason: The LGU having a hard time to strictly submit the reports on the time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended t h e Local Chief Executive (LCE) to adhere to Sections 7.1.1 and 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009 - 006 dated September 15, 2009 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer, including all Accountable Officers to: a. Remind the Local Chief Executive (LCE) about his responsibility on all government funds, required financial reports, pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009. b. Transmit immediately to the Audit Team the financial reports and unsubmitted disbursement vouchers as listed in Appendix P; otherwise, Notices of Suspension shall be issued; c. Comply strictly with the timeframes on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submission of financial reports in accordance with Section 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; and d. Impose appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned who remained unmindful of the set deadline for the submission of the accounts / reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 , otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.reason: The LGU having a hard time to strictly submit the reports on the time.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineer to submit inspection report of the projects under infrastructure services to faithfully discharge their functions and responsibilities as provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular as above for maximum benefit, pursuant to Section 476 (b) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: Inspection Reports for the projects under infrastructure services to be prepared by the Municipal Engineer
observations.reason: AS of this date, the LGU did not submit the IR to the office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation:
We commend the Management for its regular compliance with the mandatory
insurance premiums and loan amortizations due in compliance to Sections 5 (b)
and 6 of Republic Act No. 8291, otherwise known as the Government Service
Insurance System Act of 1997.

We commend the management for its effort to enforce immediate settlement of the
audit suspensions totaling to ₱ 2,741,322.42 in accordance with Section 9.1 of
Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 94-001 dated January 20, 1994.
Henceforth, ensure that government auditing rules and regulations are strictly
complied with before processing claims/effecting payments to minimize suspensions
and disallowances in audit.
Informing by the Municipal Accountant to all persons responsible and/or liable about
the suspensions on their claim and require them to submit the necessary documents
and/or justification as required in the Notice of Suspensions with a period of ninety
(90) days from receipt of the notices; otherwise the suspension shall mature into
disallowance.

We commend the Management for the implementation of the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2020 on the five percent (5%) of the total
budget and thirty percent (30%) for Quick Response Fund (QRF) in accordance
with Section 21 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 10121, otherwise known as an Act
strengthening the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction Management System,
providing for the National Disaster Risk and Reduction and Management Plan,
appropriating funds thereof and other purposes and Section 5.1.5 of the Commission
on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare, update and submit the
Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to ensure timely and accurate financial
reporting of the account Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account-111. She
should make a representation, with the approval of the Municipal Mayor, to the
agency depository bank for the timely release of the bank statements even snapshots
in order that she can prepare,update and submit the BRS on time. The Municipal
Mayor, being the head of the agency, should see to it that this requirement should be
complied by the Municipal Accountant being the responsible officer to prepare the
BRS. Moreover, she should initiate efforts to address the problem of the latter by
reducing her workloads and delegate them to other personnel capable of doing the job
in the finance department. In accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree No.
1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philipines and
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated
October 2, 1996.

We recommend that the municipality shall observe the provisions as prescribed
under Sections 182, 187 and 189 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM), for
Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. The Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer shall see to it that the SLC and SC are
reconciled for accurate recording and accounting and presentation in the financial
statement.

We recommend the LGU to create Inventory Committee to conduct Physical Count
of PPE and prepare journal entries to effect the changes, pursuant to Section 124,
Volume I and Section 57, Volume II of the Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant maintain separate ledgers for
premiums and various types of loans for the purpose of monitoring the
contributions and loan payments to Pag-IBIG to monitor the actual amounts
due; (b) make sure that the amount withheld for a given month is remitted in
full within the prescribed period so that members can enjoy optimum benefits
due them, pursuant to Section 2, Rule VII of the Implementing Rules and
Regulations (IRR) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 9679 or the Home
Development Mutual Law of 2009.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Instruct the In Charge of the Office (ICO) -Municipal Treasurer, together
with other concerned offices to (i) intensify the collection of real property
taxes, including other revenue sources to generate more income for the
Municipality; (ii) post the notice of the delinquency in publicly accessible and
conspicuous places in each barangay of the LGU concerned; and (iii)
publish the notice of delinquency once a week or for two consecutive
weeks in a newspaper of general circulation of the Municipality. Proper
information dissemination would help to inform concerned taxpayers of
their tax obligations and ultimately make them aware of their duty to pay
in accordance with Section 57 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445,
otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines;
b. Avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of the Local Government
Code of 1991 to enhance the collection of real property tax and enforce
necessary administrative action thru levy on real property or judicial
action as a last resort; and
c. Remind the In Charge of the Office (ICO) - Municipal Treasurer his
accountabilities in case of failure to collect, pursuant to Section 259 of the
Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government
Code of 1991.

We recommend to the LGU to adhere to paragraph 4.1-4.3 of the Department of
Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 125 dated
April 7, 2020 and submit utilization reports to the Audit Team such as
Disbursement Vouchers (DVs), Report of Check Issued (RCIs) and other related
reports for audit purposes.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to adhere to Sections 7.1.1
and 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009 - 006 dated
September 15, 2009 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting
Municipal Treasurer, including all Accountable Officers to:
a. Remind the Local Chief Executive (LCE) about his responsibility on all
government funds, property, required financial reports, pursuant to Section
7.1.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September 15, 2009.
b. Transmit immediately to the Audit Team the financial reports and
unsubmitted disbursement vouchers as listed in Appendix P; otherwise,
Notices of Suspension shall be issued;
c. Comply strictly with the timeframes on the rendition of accounts and
preparation/submission of financial reports in accordance with Section 7.2.1 of
the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009; and
d. Impose appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned
who remained unmindful of the set deadline for the submission of the
accounts / reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No.
1445 , otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.

Require the Municipal Engineer to submit inspection report of the projects under
infrastructure services to faithfully discharge their functions and responsibilities as
provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular as above mentioned
for maximum benefit, pursuant to Section 476 (b) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160,
otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare, update and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting of the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account – 111. She should make a representation, with the approval of the Municipal Mayor, to the agency depository bank for the timely release of the bank statements even snapshots in order that she can prepare, update and submit the BRS on time. The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, should see to it that this requirement should be complied by the Municipal Accountant being the responsible officer to prepare the BRS. Moreover, she should initiate efforts to address the problem of the latter by reducing her workloads and delegate them to other personnel capable of doing the job in the finance department in accordance with Section 74 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines, Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-125A dated March 4, 1992 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction:
Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) for the periods January 2020 to October 2020 for the Noted Cash balances were prepared and submitted to the Commission on Audit (COA). The accounting department is still preparing the BRS for the periods November 2020 to December 2020 and the same will be submitted to COA on or before April 30, 2021.
The Accounting Department will exert much efforts to submit Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) to the office of the Auditor on period prescribed in Circular No. 96-11 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: The bank reconciliation statement submitted after the reporting time statement.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to (a) capitalize the procured Motor Vehicles (MVs) to the amounting the procured motor vehicle; (b) recognize Depreciation Expense for the two MV’s; (c) conduct Physical Count of PPE; and (d) prepare journal entries to effect the changes, pursuant to Section 63 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) NO. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines, paragraph 71 and Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) 4.5 and Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17 – Property, Plant and Equipment and Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-836 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 124 and 57 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume 1.
The Municipal Accountant shall capitalize the procured motor vehicles and recognize depreciation expense using the Straight Line Method and conduct the Physical Count of PPE to arrive at a reliable value of the PPE in the Financial Statement.
observations.managementAction: The LGU had prepared jounal entries for the capitalization of the Motor Vehicles (MVs)
observations.reason: The Agency failed to conduct the physical count

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management (a) continually exert effort to comply with Section 5 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 8291, otherwise known as the Revised Government Service Insurance Act of 1997 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) on the withholding and remittance of premium contributions and loan amortizations of employees; and (b) instruct the concerned personnel to determine the causes of over or under remittance and determine the exact amount to be remitted to arrive at an accurate balance of the Due to GSIS at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum of Agreement had been entered between LGU South Upi and GSIS granting a sixty (60%) Restructured Obligation within a period of twelve (12) months, with interest of twelve percent (12%) per annum, diminishing balance, payable on a monthly basis. Monthly payments had been made by the LGU starting October 2019.

observations.recommendation:
Enforce immediate settlement of the audit suspensions totaling to ₱1349,531.29 in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009 – 006 dated September 15, 2009. Henceforth, ensure that government auditing rules and regulations are strictly complied with before processing claims/effecting payments to minimize suspensions and disallowances in audit.
The Municipal Accountant should inform all persons responsible and/or liable about the suspension on their claim and require them to submit the necessary documents and/or justification as required in the Notice of Suspensions with a period of ninety (90) days from receipt of the notices; otherwise the suspension shall mature into disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Inform the responsible person/s about in accordance with Section 2 and require the necessary documents to settle the suspension in accordance with Section 9.4 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009 – 006 dated September 15,2009.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to adhere to Sections 7.1.1 and 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009 - 006 dated September 15, 2009 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer, including all Accountable Officers to:

Recommendation
DirecttheMunicipalAccountant to(a) capitalizetheprocured MotorVehicles
(MVs) amounting to P3,075,000; (b) recognize Depreciation Expense for the
two MVs; (c) conduct Physical Count of PPE ; and (d) prepare journal
entries to effect the changes, pursuant to Section 63 of the Presidential Decree
(P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines, paragraph 71 and Philippine Application Guidance (PAG) 4,5 and
6 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard No. 17 - Property, Plant
and Equipment (PPE) and Section 156 of the Commission of Audit (COA)
Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Sections 124 and 57 of the
Manual in the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local
GovernmentUnits,Volume I.
The Municipal Accountant shall capitalize the procured motor vehicles and
recognize depreciation expense using the Straight Line Method and conduct
the Physical Count of PPE to arrive at a reliable value of the PPE in the
FinancialStatement.
Thecomputationofdepreciation expense:
AcquisitionCost =P3,075,000
EstimatedUseful LifeofMV=7
ResidualValue =P3,075,000 x5% =P153,750
Carrying Amount=P3,075,000 x95%=P2,921,250
Annual Depreciation = P2,921,250=P417,321.43
7
Monthly Depreciation = P417,321.43/12x2= P69,553.57
a) The journal entry totake upthe depreciation expense:
Depreciation Expense P69,553.57
Accumulated Depreciation P69,553.57
Torecord the depreciation charged for two months for CY 2019
b) The journal entry totake upthe net bookvalue of the MVs:
DRR Vehicle-Motor Vehicle P2,842,716.43
Government Equity P2,842,716.43
To bookupthe unrecorded purchase ofmotor vehicle

Recommendation
We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to adhere to Sections
7.1.1 and 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009 - 006
dated September 15, 2009 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the
Acting Municipal Treasurer, including all Accountable Officers to:
a. Remind the Local Chief Executive (LCE) about his responsibility on all
government funds, property, required financial reports, pursuant to Section
7.1.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated
September15,2009.
b. Transmit immediately to the Audit Team the financial reports and
unsubmitted disbursement vouchers as listed in Appendix P; otherwise,
Notices ofSuspension shall beissued;
c. Comply strictly with the timeframes on the rendition of accounts and
preparation/submission of financial reports in accordance with Section 7.2.1 of
the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009;
d. Impose appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned
who remained unmindful of the set deadline for the submission of the
accounts/reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.)
No. 1445 , otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.

Recommendation
We recommended that management (a) continually abide with the BIR
regulation on the deadline of remittance of taxes withheld from employees as
well as from suppliers/contractors; (b) instruct the personnel in-charge to
determine the exact amount to be remitted to arrive at an accurate balance
of the Due to BIR at year-end; and (c) determine the causes of over or under
remittance that eventually distorted the account balance and coordinate with
the BIR as regards possible offsetting of the excessive remittances against
the amounts due in the succeeding remittance period, pursuant to 4.114 (c) of
the Revenue Regulations No. 2-98 dated August 17, 1998 and Section 5 of
RevenueRegulations No.17-2003.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare and update the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting of the account Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current Account-111. She should make a representation, with the approval of the Municipal Mayor, to the agency depository bank for the timely release of the bank statements in order that she can prepare and update the BRS on time. The Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, should see to it that this requirement should be complied by the Accountant being the responsible officer to prepare the BRS. Moreover, he should initiate efforts to address the problem of the latter by reducing his workloads and delegate them to other personnel capable of doing the job in the finance department.

observations.recommendation: Enforce immediate settlement of the audit suspensions totaling ₱1,589,151.87 in accordance with Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 94-001 dated January 20, 1994. The Municipal Accountant should inform all persons responsible and/or liable about the suspensions on their claim and require them to submit the necessary documents and/or justification as required in the Notice of Suspensions with a period of ninety (90) days from receipt of the notices; otherwise the suspension shall mature into disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

        Enforce immediate  settlement  of  the  audit  suspensions  totaling
        P1,679,700.78 in accordance with Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 94-
        001 dated January  20,  1994.  Henceforth,  ensure  that  government
        auditing rules and regulations are  strictly  complied  with  before
        processing claims/effecting payments  to  minimize  suspensions  and
        disallowances in audit.
                                    
        The Municipal  Accountant  should  inform  all  persons  responsible
        and/or liable about the suspensions on their claim and require  them
        to submit the necessary documents and/or justification  as  required
        in the Notice of Suspensions with a period of ninety (90) days  from
        receipt of the notices; otherwise the suspension shall  mature  into
        disallowance.

We recommend to the Local Chief  Executive  to  instruct  the  General
     Service Officer to reconcile the PPE account with the books  of  account
     and assigned book value thereof in accordance with section 124 of the
     Manual  of New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.

Recommendation:

        We recommend that all deductions  due  to  GSIS  withheld  from  the
        salaries of     employees be remitted on time  to  avoid  penalties,
        surcharges, and accumulations of monthly dues.

Recommendation

    We recommend and management agreed  to  strictly  monitor  and  evaluate
    regularly the implementation of projects through the Provincial Planning
    and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer and instruct  the
    implementing officer to fast track project implementation.

    Ensure also that preliminary detailed engineering study and  design  are
    properly executed to minimized, if not totally  avoid  variation  orders
    and time extension.

Recommendation

    We recommend that management procure their commonly used supplies  from
    the Procurement Service of  the  DBM   or  through  public  bidding  in
    compliance with above-cited law. Emergency items that are not available
    from the Procurement Service  maybe purchased  thru  shopping  provided
    that procurement is supported with BAC Resolution recommending  to  the
    Head of the Procuring Agency (HOPE) that the mode  of  procurement  for
    those items shall  be  thru  shopping  and  a  certification  from  the
    Procurement Service that the items requested are not available.


      Management justification

    Management explained that their procurement of commonly  used  supplies
    were not in  huge  volume  that  purchasing  it  from  the  Procurement
    Services in would entail other cost/charges such as travelling expenses
    and freight.

Recommendation

      We recommend that management avail of the services provided by the LBP
      through  an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) for payment of salaries  to
      its permanent employees as well as casual and job order  personnel  in
      order to  promote  operational  efficiency  as  well  as  strengthened
      internal control on cash and to avoid further  the  risk  of  possible
      loss/misapplication.

We recommend that the agency shall prepare and maintain the Registries required, and submit the Status Report and List of Infrastructures Projects Completed by the Municipal Engineer to support the existence of the account.  Also, the Accounting Unit shall maintain the PPE Ledger Card by category of assets for proper recording and facilitate our verification.
We recommend that the Treasurer shall furnish the Chief Accountant of a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year.
We recommend that all deductions due to GSIS withheld from the salaries of employees be remitted on time to avoid penalties, surcharges, and accumulations of monthly dues.
observations.reason: They are implemting remittance thru MOA

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the required information and records such as the Sangguniang Resolution approving the loan, Annual Investment Plan (AIP) that includes programs/projects to be funded from the proceeds of the loan, copy of loan and credit financing agreement with the lender, supporting schedules and document such as Individual Loan Profile, and general ledger of the account Loans Payable-Domestic 444 be submitted to verify its correctness and validity.

We recommend that the LGU shall submit the disbursement vouchers for the period July to December, 2014 for post audit and examination. Continued disregard will be dealt with accordingly pursuant to the above stated provision of PD 1445.
